data.current_summary: Schedule SQL query as long-running job
data.current_description: Schedule SQL query as long-running job
data.description_suggestion: Schedules a read-only SQL query to execute asynchronously as a long-running job. Returns a job ID that can be used to poll for status or cancel the operation. Useful for complex queries that may exceed synchronous timeout limits. Subject to RBAC.
data.reasoning: Current description is repetitive and lacks detail. Improved version clarifies that it's read-only (consistent with executeQuery), explains the return value (job ID), provides context for when to use async queries, and mentions RBAC constraints for consistency.

message: Operation 'getAsyncQueryStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description is minimal and repetitive. Improved version explains the input requirement (job ID), describes possible status values, clarifies when results are available, and provides context for how to use this operation in a workflow.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status of a long-running asynchronous query job. Requires the job ID returned from scheduleAsyncQuery. Returns job status (pending, running, completed, failed) and results if available. Use this to poll for completion before retrieving final results.
data.reasoning: Current description is minimal and repetitive. Improved version explains the input requirement (job ID), describes possible status values, clarifies when results are available, and provides context for how to use this operation in a workflow.

message: Operation 'cancelAsyncQuery' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is minimal. Improved version clarifies the input requirement (job ID), explains the side effects (stops execution, prevents retrieval), describes the return value, and notes that cancellation is permanent for AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Cancels a long-running asynchronous query job. Requires the job ID returned from scheduleAsyncQuery. Stops query execution if still running and prevents result retrieval. Returns confirmation of cancellation. Cancelled jobs cannot be resumed.
data.reasoning: Current description is minimal. Improved version clarifies the input requirement (job ID), explains the side effects (stops execution, prevents retrieval), describes the return value, and notes that cancellation is permanent for AI agent decision-making.

data.summary_suggestion: Remove user account from CFEngine
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a user account from the CFEngine Mission Portal. Requires the username parameter. This operation removes all user credentials and RBAC role assignments. The deleted user will no longer be able to authenticate or access the system. This action cannot be undone.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new RBAC role in the CFEngine Mission Portal with the specified roleId and configuration. Accepts a RoleRequest body containing role name, description, and permission definitions. Returns the created role object. The new role becomes immediately available for user assignment. Duplicate roleIds will be rejected with a conflict error.

data.summary_suggestion: Remove RBAC role from system
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes an RBAC role identified by roleId from the CFEngine Mission Portal. Requires the roleId path parameter. This operation removes the role definition and all associated permission mappings. Users currently assigned to this role will lose the permissions granted by it. This action cannot be undone. Verify role usage before deletion.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all managed hosts with optional filtering
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all hosts managed by CFEngine Enterprise. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns a collection of host objects containing host identifiers, names, status, and other inventory attributes. Useful for discovering available hosts and their current state.
data.reasoning: Original description is extremely vague with no context about parameters, filtering capabilities, or response structure. AI agents need to understand what filtering options are available (4 parameters mentioned) and what data is returned to determine if this operation matches their intent.

message: Operation 'getHost' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description lacks specificity about what 'details' are returned and the structure of the response. data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve detailed information for a specific host
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific host identified by hostId. Returns a HostObject containing host identification, status, inventory attributes, and operational metrics. Use this operation to obtain complete information about a particular managed host.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what 'details' are returned and the structure of the response. AI agents need to know the response schema (HostObject) and what information is available to determine if this operation provides the data they need.

Description mentions Ansible compatibility but lacks details about response structure, parameter options (4 parameters available), and use cases. data.current_summary: List hosts grouped by hard classes
data.current_description: Returns hosts grouped by their CFEngine classes (Ansible inventory compatible).
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves hosts organized by their CFEngine hard classes, formatted in Ansible inventory-compatible structure. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns a hierarchical collection where hosts are grouped by class name. Useful for infrastructure organization and cross-tool compatibility.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description mentions Ansible compatibility but lacks details about response structure, parameter options (4 parameters available), and use cases. message: Operation 'listDeletedHosts' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description provides no context about the state of deleted hosts, whether they can be recovered, or what information is available. AI agents need to understand that deleted hosts are recoverable and what options exist for managing them.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve list of deleted hosts with restoration options
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of hosts that have been deleted but not permanently removed from the system. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns deleted host objects with deletion timestamps and metadata. Deleted hosts can be restored using the restore-deleted endpoint or permanently removed.
data.reasoning: Original description provides no context about the state of deleted hosts, whether they can be recovered, or what information is available. AI agents need to understand that deleted hosts are recoverable and what options exist for managing them.

message: Operation 'restoreDeletedHost' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks any context about what restoration means operationally. AI agents need to understand the state transition, side effects (report collection resumes), and what is returned to determine if this operation achieves their goal.

data.summary_suggestion: Restore a previously deleted host to active management
data.description_suggestion: Restores a previously deleted host identified by hostId back to active management status. The host will resume reporting and be included in standard host listings. Returns the restored HostObject with updated status. Side effect: Host transitions from deleted state to active state and report collection resumes.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks any context about what restoration means operationally. AI agents need to understand the state transition, side effects (report collection resumes), and what is returned to determine if this operation achieves their goal.

message: Operation 'permanentlyDeleteHost' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original description does not convey the irreversible nature or severity of this operation. AI agents need clear warnings about permanence and inability to recover to avoid accidental data loss. The description should emphasize this is a destructive operation distinct from the soft delete operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Permanently remove host from system with no recovery option
data.description_suggestion: Permanently removes a deleted host identified by hostId from the system with no recovery option. This operation is irreversible and removes all associated host data, history, and records. Use only when you are certain the host should be completely removed. Side effects: Host data is permanently deleted and cannot be restored.
data.reasoning: Original description does not convey the irreversible nature or severity of this operation. AI agents need clear warnings about permanence and inability to recover to avoid accidental data loss. The description should emphasize this is a destructive operation distinct from the soft delete operation.

Operation 'scheduleAsyncQuery' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description is repetitive and lacks detail. Improved version clarifies that it's read-only (consistent with executeQuery), explains the return value (job ID), provides context for when to use async queries, and mentions RBAC constraints for consistency.

data.current_summary: Schedule SQL query as long-running job
data.current_description: Schedule SQL query as long-running job
data.description_suggestion: Schedules a read-only SQL query to execute asynchronously as a long-running job. Returns a job ID that can be used to poll for status or cancel the operation. Useful for complex queries that may exceed synchronous timeout limits. Subject to RBAC.
data.reasoning: Current description is repetitive and lacks detail. Improved version clarifies that it's read-only (consistent with executeQuery), explains the return value (job ID), provides context for when to use async queries, and mentions RBAC constraints for consistency.

message: Operation 'getAsyncQueryStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description is minimal and repetitive. Improved version explains the input requirement (job ID), describes possible status values, clarifies when results are available, and provides context for how to use this operation in a workflow.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status of a long-running asynchronous query job. Requires the job ID returned from scheduleAsyncQuery. Returns job status (pending, running, completed, failed) and results if available. Use this to poll for completion before retrieving final results.
data.reasoning: Current description is minimal and repetitive. Improved version explains the input requirement (job ID), describes possible status values, clarifies when results are available, and provides context for how to use this operation in a workflow.

message: Operation 'cancelAsyncQuery' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is minimal. Improved version clarifies the input requirement (job ID), explains the side effects (stops execution, prevents retrieval), describes the return value, and notes that cancellation is permanent for AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Cancels a long-running asynchronous query job. Requires the job ID returned from scheduleAsyncQuery. Stops query execution if still running and prevents result retrieval. Returns confirmation of cancellation. Cancelled jobs cannot be resumed.
data.reasoning: Current description is minimal. Improved version clarifies the input requirement (job ID), explains the side effects (stops execution, prevents retrieval), describes the return value, and notes that cancellation is permanent for AI agent decision-making.
